key_insight: Mn substitution at the Ni site in Cu2NiXY4 kesterites preserves the tetragonal crystal structure while systematically narrowing the bandgap through d–p orbital hybridization.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical composition-tuning route for designing kesterite semiconductors with adjustable electronic properties for optoelectronic devices.
method: First-principles DFT study of Cu2NiXY4 (X = Sn, Ge, Si; Y = S, Se) compounds.
Applied the mBJ+U approach to improve bandgap estimation.
Compared pristine and 50% Mn-substituted Ni-site configurations across the full family.
evidence_signal: Across the studied compounds, the bandgap decreased from 1.028–3.397 eV to 1.007–3.333 eV, with Cu2NiSnS4 dropping from 1.59 eV to 1.49 eV.
watch_item: The reported Mn substitution corresponds to a high 50% concentration in the unit cell, so lower-doping behavior will require supercell calculations and may differ.
paper_title: First-principles Study of Structural and Electronic Properties of Mn-doped Cu2NiXY4 (X=Sn, Ge, Si; Y=S, Se) Chalcogenide Semiconductors
